concept_decisions.retained_canonical_name: Genomic epidemiology, phylodynamics, and related transmission methods
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: all titles and complete available abstracts in CONCEPT-BATCH-004_RECORDS.csv plus concept counts in CONCEPT-BATCH-004.csv
concept_decisions.rationale: The only assigned record is a genome-scale phylodynamic spatiotemporal reconstruction of lineage spread, not a distinct Bayesian spatiotemporal disease-model concept.

concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: all titles and complete available abstracts in CONCEPT-BATCH-004_RECORDS.csv plus concept counts in CONCEPT-BATCH-004.csv
concept_decisions.rationale: Most records are still genuinely network-based infectious-disease models, but a substantial subset are Bayesian belief networks or neural-network prediction systems with different estimands and identification assumptions.

concept_decisions.retained_canonical_name: Genomic epidemiology, phylodynamics, and related transmission methods
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: all titles and complete available abstracts in CONCEPT-BATCH-004_RECORDS.csv plus concept counts in CONCEPT-BATCH-004.csv
concept_decisions.rationale: The eight assigned records are genomic origin, hotspot, phylogeographic, or molecular-network studies rather than descriptive spatial clustering analyses.

concept_decisions.retained_canonical_name: Genomic epidemiology, phylodynamics, and related transmission methods
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: all titles and complete available abstracts in CONCEPT-BATCH-004_RECORDS.csv plus concept counts in CONCEPT-BATCH-004.csv
concept_decisions.rationale: No assigned record is a true ecological-niche/species-distribution design; the set splits between sequence-informed diffusion/phylodynamic studies and population-genomic/within-host evolutionary studies.

concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: all titles and complete available abstracts in CONCEPT-BATCH-004_RECORDS.csv plus concept counts in CONCEPT-BATCH-004.csv
concept_decisions.rationale: All eight records use the endemic-epidemic/HHH class or direct bounded-count extensions of that framework.

concept_decisions.retained_canonical_name: Genomic epidemiology, phylodynamics, and related transmission methods
concept_decisions.labels_compared: genomic_epidemiology_transmission with merged genomic-like records from neighboring provisional labels
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: all titles and complete available abstracts in CONCEPT-BATCH-004_RECORDS.csv plus concept counts in CONCEPT-BATCH-004.csv
concept_decisions.rationale: The dominant cluster consistently uses sequence data to infer transmission origin, spread, lineage movement, outbreak structure, or phylodynamic history.

concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: all titles and complete available abstracts in CONCEPT-BATCH-004_RECORDS.csv plus concept counts in CONCEPT-BATCH-004.csv
concept_decisions.rationale: The assigned records remain centered on gravity-style or mobility-interaction structure, including epidemiologic and risk-distribution applications.

concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: all titles and complete available abstracts in CONCEPT-BATCH-004_RECORDS.csv plus concept counts in CONCEPT-BATCH-004.csv
concept_decisions.rationale: Most records are genuine patch-structured transmission models, but a clear subset instead studies host-pathogen metapopulation ecology/evolution or sequence-based phylogeography/phylodynamics.

reassignments.rationale: Phylodynamic spatiotemporal reconstruction of lineage spread, not a standalone Bayesian spatial disease-model concept.

new_concepts.rationale: Records in this split estimate conditional dependence or risk structure with Bayesian networks rather than simulate transmission on contact or mobility graphs.
These records use ANN/CNN/GCN/deep-learning systems for diagnosis, classification, or forecasting rather than mechanistic epidemic-network dynamics.

new_concepts.canonical_name: Pathogen population genomics and within-host evolution
new_concepts.rationale: This concept captures records whose estimand is sequence diversity, pan-genome structure, or within-host variant emergence rather than transmission spread.
This split captures ecological, evolutionary, wildlife-management, and genetic-structure studies framed at metapopulation scale but not as mechanistic patch-transmission models.
